reviews.review_description: This app is going to change the way students learn math forever. Prisms is making sure all students get a chance to experience math the way it should be taught and making it fun through real-life experiences.

Amazing! The days of giving someone a VR headset and have them learn any topic in the coolest possible classroom in the world are here, starting with 8-12 grade Math. Each lesson has been beautifully built up by starting with a real-life problem (why do I need this? Something that's often missing in the "real school") and then learning the topic in small manageable pieces, with lots of interaction. There are 21 modules at launch, plus a few multiplayer spaces. It's $24 for an annual subscription, with free content and further improvements surely coming. Great job, I can't wait for more, and will recommend it excitedly to everyone learning Math! 👏

reviews.review_title: Great first start at Math in VR
reviews.review_description:
It's exciting to see a math game in VR that is fully experiential and allows you to see practical use cases for complex math upfront. I jumped to advanced algebra and got to travel underwater to determine how to create an optimized underwater cabling system for transmitting data. I can't say I did that in school! 

Overall, I think the application has a lot of potential to grow, and this is just the beginning. I hope the developers, over time, continue to refine the overall user experience starting with teleportation. I would love to see a laser pointer for selecting or pressing buttons versus always having to teleport and physically push my hand in certain instances. I also think implementing a gamification/rewards system would greatly incentivize players to keep going on their math journey. 

Super excited to see how this app evolves!

Learning Math in VR seems daunting at first just coz it’s math but it’s easier to understand the lessons since it is tied to real world applications, and not just looking at numbers in a piece of paper. I can definitely see how this can be adapted in a classroom setting and beyond. Wish I had this when I was in middle school, high school. 

Though the 3D models and textures could be further improved. The topics were well communicated visually.
App does not go past the first screen

Prisms is a stellar example of how complex things can be taught in VR. Connecting mathematical concepts to real world problems, and then being able to experience them in VR, makes the learning process super gratifying, and the learnings more memorable. Next time I think about a circle's radius, I'm sure the image of a yurt will pop up into my head. 

Apps like these make me excited for the future of education.

What an incredible tool for the classroom. From the tutorial, all the way through the modules you're engaged and immersed in what you're learning. Throughout these experiences, I caught myself wishing this was available when I was learning these concepts...But, it's here now, and I'm thrilled. Hats off to Prisms!

It’s an educational application and it’s true the price is a Barrier you should have left some free options to give a taste to the game. But it remains a passable Barrier I find that education has a cost, which must remain accessible but a cost only at the same. For me the big obstacle is the language please add at least one other language, namely French!
If you do not accept payment cards that Meta accepts (and that works fine in store) add another payment method or repair in-app purchase system to accept secure payments.

Been trying to get the annual subscription.
The transaction gets approved but once I go to the app it just says that the transaction has been  canceled from the app.

reviews.review_title: Studied math at university but no clue
reviews.review_description: ... how to play and what to do at Basic algebra. There are 2 options: I am a total idiot or people who created game mechanic are autistic.

I have two headsets under the same meta account.  One for my daughter and for myself.  I purchased the subscription on one. I am unable to buy a new subscription on another, nor I am able to continue without.  I wanted to create a multi-player session to help if needed.  

Now I am unsure how to contact for help

My son loves it. Thank you for making an educational game! Shared with educators and homeschoolers. 
Happy to pay for REAL CONTENT. Keep up the good work and please make more. 10 stars.

I had a chance to work with the algebra > linear functions simulation which brought immersion, interactivity, and a quiet space to think things through.  The simulation complimented Module 4, Lesson 2 & 3 of the McGraw-HIll Reveal Integrated Math 1 curriculum.

As a community college librarian who utilizes VR with faculty and students, I've been searching for a math app to make that connection with instructors. I'd spoken with someone with Prisms in the fall of 2022 about content that may be coming in early 2023 -- I'm so excited the app has launched!

I've only completed the Fractions section(s) thus far, but I am amazed at how interactive, functional, and concrete the sections were. I've already reached out to my math faculty asking them to stop by and try the different sections since they are the content experts -- I really see this as a game changer for math education. 

If I get instructor feedback that changes my rating I'll update my review, but after trying Pandemic (Exponential Functions) in the App Lab and Fractions in the full app, I can't imagine the other sections will be anything short of excellent.

Please keep making more educational content like this!
There is no option to cancel this subscription and i want to cancel it

I'm a 15 year high school math teacher that got to demo this recently. I immediately purchased a used quest 2 for this app for my middle schooler and elementary schooler that we homeschool. Works amazingly well, is intuitive and really reinforces our home curriculum. My oldest son sat voluntarily in a chair on a weekend for an hour and learned how to do surface area with rectangle, triangle and trapezoidal faces on 3d solids. He hadn't done surface area before, only some area of 2d shapes. He was able to complete the module with only a few corrections for wrong work. 

Disclaimer: I was watching him complete it screencasting his work on my phone and helped him with knowing what tools were available to him to solve, and help him with questions he has about the work. But I let him make mistakes and figure out what he did incorrectly on his own. I'm certified to teach secondary math. With that being said, I believe this app could be revolutionary in the right hands. 

I only wish that this version of the app was still being updated to include all the new modules being added to the school district version, including the sciences. 

Prisms, please consider supporting this consumer version again.
havent used this spp in months and thought subscription was cancelled but still charged

reviews.review_title: One of the best ways to learn Math!
reviews.review_description: As a former Physics teacher I really could have used this with my students!  Vectors are a very difficult topic and difficult to teach in a "traditional" manner.  Prisms changes that completely!  You are immersed in a VR world and presented with a practical application to using vectors.  Then being able to interact with the graph, the guidance by an "instructor", the ability to use a virtual calculator... after 5 minutes you forget you are sitting in your living room!!  I cannot wait to try the other advanced modules.  I will also get this for my grandaughters... they will learn Algebra before entering the 8th grade!!  AWESOME!!
Make it free because it someone got a meta quest and has diffrent money they cant buy it
